18 May 2026 Military News

Operations

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • US-Nigeria Coordinated Strike Against ISIS Fighters AFRICOM 18 May 2026 -- On May 17, 2026, U.S. Africa Command, in coordination with the Government of Nigeria, conducted additional kinetic strikes against ISIS in Northeastern Nigeria.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • May 18: Iran reaffirms national resolve, advances new diplomatic and maritime actions Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Iran on Monday reaffirmed its national resolve against any form of foreign aggression, with President Masoud Pezeshkian stating that the country will not compromise its dignity even as officials advanced new diplomatic steps and maritime initiatives despite continued US maritime banditry and sabre-rattling.
  • Iran, Oman crafting new transit mechanism for Strait of Hormuz: Foreign Ministry Press TV 18 May 2026 -- The spokesman for Iran's Foreign Ministry says the country is engaged in continuous consultations with Oman to design a new mechanism governing transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

  • Trump Says He's Paused Planned Iran Strike As Peace Negotiations Continue RFE/RL 18 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump says he has paused a planned military strike on Iran to allow negotiations over ending the conflict in the Middle East, after Tehran submitted a new peace proposal through Pakistan.
  • Trump Says 'Not Open' to Any Concessions After Iran's Latest Peace Proposal Sputnik 18 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Monday that he was "not open" to any concessions after receiving Iran's latest peace proposal.

  • Netanyahu, Trump discuss possible renewed fighting against Iran over phone -- state media People's Daily 18 May 2026 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and U.S. President Donald Trump on Sunday discussed the possibility of renewing fighting against Iran over phone, Israel's state-owned Kan TV news reported.

  • Iranian Official Downplays Seriousness Of Mojtaba Khamenei's Injuries RFE/RL 18 May 2026 -- An Iranian Health Ministry official has dismissed reports that Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei suffered debilitating injuries during an Israeli strike on the Tehran residence of his father, Ali Khamenei, insisting that "nothing significant" happened to him.
  • Iranian nation and armed forces will force US retreat and surrender: Leader's advisor Press TV 18 May 2026 -- A senior advisor to Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei has stated that the iron fist of Iran's powerful armed forces and the resolute Iranian nation will compel the United States to retreat and surrender.
  • Iran says any agreement with US must end war on all fronts, lift blockade and sanctions Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi has said that any possible agreement with the US must ensure an end to the war on all fronts and lift the naval blockade and sanctions imposed against the country.
  • Iran submits new 14-point proposal to US via Pakistan to end war: Report Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Iran has reportedly submitted its latest proposal in 14 points through the Pakistani intermediary for talks with the United States aimed at ending the illegal US-Israeli war of aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • 'We will never bow': Pezeshkian vows to fight on amid Trump's escalating war rhetoric Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian has vowed never to yield to any power or sacrifice the nation's dignity for comfort.
  • Iran FM discusses regional issues with four counterparts amid US escalations Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi held separate telephone conversations with his counterparts from France, Qatar, Turkey, and South Korea on Sunday to discuss bilateral issues, regional developments, and ongoing diplomatic processes.
  • Iran Ready for Any Scenario of Conflict With US - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 18 May 2026 -- Iran is prepared for any scenario in its conflict with the United States, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei said on Monday.

  • 'Only weeks' of commercial oil inventories left, IEA chief warns Press TV 18 May 2026 -- The head of the International Energy Agency (IEA) has warned that commercial oil inventories are depleting rapidly and that the world only has a few weeks' worth of oil reserves left due to the situation in the Strait of Hormuz caused by the US-Israeli aggression against Iran.

  • Former Pentagon chief says he dismissed Netanyahu's 'dead wrong' prediction on Iran war Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Former Pentagon chief and CIA director Robert Gates says he rejected an assessment by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu suggesting that Iran's government would rapidly collapse if subjected to military aggression.

  • UN underscores protection of nuclear sites following drone strike on Emirati plant UN News 18 May 2026 -- The head of the UN atomic watchdog on Monday underscored the need to safeguard nuclear security in wartime, a day after a drone strike near a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates (UAE).
  • Statement by the Secretary-General on the attack near the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant UN Secretary-General 18 May 2026
  • IAEA chief calls for maximum military restraint near nuclear power plant People's Daily 18 May 2026 -- The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) chief expressed "grave concern" over the Sunday drone strike which caused a fire near the Barakah nuclear plant in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), calling for maximum restraint to avoid a nuclear accident.

  • Pakistan Strongly Condemns the Drone Attack on the Barakah Nuclear Energy Plant of the United Arab Emirates Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 18 May 2026 -- Pakistan strongly condemns the drone attack on the Barakah Nuclear Energy Plant in the United Arab Emirates on 17 May 2026. Pakistan stands in full solidarity with the brotherly people and Government of the UAE.

  • Aboul Gheit condemns drone attack on Saudi Arabia League of Arab States 18 May 2026 -- Mr. Ahmed Aboul Gheit, Secretary-General of the League of Arab States, condemned in the strongest terms the blatant attack carried out against the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia by three drones that violated Saudi airspace in a flagrant breach of the Kingdom's sovereignty and territorial integrity, affirming that this cowardly attack cannot be accepted or justified under any circumstances.
  • The General Secretariat of the OIC Condemns the Penetration of Saudi Airspace by Drones Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 18 May 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has expressed its condemnation of the penetration of the airspace of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia by three drones on Sunday morning, 17 May 2026, in a flagrant violation of the sovereignty of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Joint Interagency Task Force 401 Awards $500 Million Counter-UAS Contract DoD News 18 May 2026 -- Joint Interagency Task Force 401 has awarded a three-year Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity contract with a $500 million ceiling to support enterprisewide counter-unmanned aerial system operations.
  • Fueling the Fight: USNS Kanawha Completes Strategic Deployment DoD News 18 May 2026 -- At long last, the familiar blue and yellow stripes around the top of the main smokestack appeared against the coastal sky, marking the successful completion of months of dedication and support to U.S. naval forces.
  • 7th Communication Battalion Marines sustain command and control during Kaiju Rain 26 USMC News 18 May 2026 -- Marines with 7th Communication Battalion, III Marine Expeditionary Force Information Group, established a communication node at Camp Shields after displacing from Marine Corps Air Station Futenma during Kaiju Rain 26, May 8, 2026.
  • USS Howard departs Shimoda, following Black Ship Festival U.S. 7th Fleet 18 May 2026 -- Forward-deployed Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer USS Howard (DDG 83) departed Shimoda after a port visit, May 17, following participation in the city's Black Ship Festival.
  • Accelerating the fight for drone dominance US Air Force 18 May 2026 -- In July 2025, the Secretary of War released a memorandum concerning the urgency of establishing U.S. military drone dominance. In it, Secretary Pete Hegseth described drones as the "biggest battlefield innovation in a generation" and called for the removal of red tape and the allocation of resources to bolster the U.S. military's ability to employ and counter drone technology. As the conflicts in Ukraine and Iran have progressed, the development of proper offensive and defensive capabilities has become even more imperative.
  • USS Cleveland (LCS 31) Commissions in Namesake City US Navy 18 May 2026 -- The U.S. Navy commissioned its newest and last Freedom-variant littoral combat ship USS Cleveland (LCS 31) in Cleveland.
  • USS Cooperstown (LCS 23) Departs Mayport for Deployment US Navy 18 May 2026 -- The Freedom-variant littoral combat ship USS Cooperstown (LCS 23) got underway from Naval Station Mayport May 16, 2026, to begin operations in support of its scheduled deployment. Cooperstown assumed duties previously executed by the Freedom-variant littoral combat ship USS Wichita (LCS 13) in support of USNORTHCOM's border security objectives.
  • U.S. Navy Launches High-Tech Unmanned Systems During Bilateral Exercise with Norway US Navy 18 May 2026 -- U.S. Navy expeditionary forces are operating in the High North to deploy and test advanced Robotics and Autonomous Systems (RAS) May 4-24, 2026, during an unmanned systems and explosive ordnance disposal EOD exercise with Norway, strengthening their bilateral posture in the Arctic.
  • Future USS Ted Stevens (DDG 128) Arrives at Homeport for the First Time US Navy 18 May 2026 -- The future USS Ted Stevens (DDG 128), a flight III Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer, arrived at its Naval Station Norfolk homeport for the first time on May 15, 2026.

  • NATO Allies strengthen interoperability during NATO Tiger Meet 2026 NATO Allied Air Command 18 May 2026 -- From May 4 to 15, 2026, aircraft and personnel from across the Alliance gathered at Araxos Air Base in Greece for Exercise NATO Tiger Meet 2026, hosted by 335 Squadron of the Hellenic Air Force.
  • Silent hunters: NATO trains for undersea warfare in the high north NATO Allied Maritime Command 18 May 2026 -- Beneath the cold waters of the Norwegian Sea, Allied submarines are manoeuvring into position for the start of one of NATO's most demanding maritime exercises. From 18 - 29 May 26, Dynamic Mongoose 26 brings together cutting-edge submarine and anti-submarine warfare (ASW) capabilities in a complex training environment, designed to put the Alliance to the test.
  • Data on interceptions of aircraft completed near the Baltic States' borders on May 11-17, 2026 Lithuania MND 18 May 2026

  • Russia, China Developing Next-Gen Heavy Helicopter Sputnik 18 May 2026 -- This landmark project will deliver China a modern heavy helicopter tailored to its needs.
  • Belarus Begins Drills of Units for Use of Nuclear Weapons, Support - Defense Ministry Sputnik 18 May 2026 -- Military units of Belarus for the combat use of nuclear weapons and nuclear support have started military drills, the Belarusian Defense Ministry said on Monday.

  • Ukraine's first glide bomb ready for combat use Ukraine MOD 18 May 2026 -- Ukraine's first glide bomb, developed by a Brave1 participant, has passed all required tests and is ready for combat deployment.
  • Statement of the MFA of Ukraine on the Belarusian-Russian Nuclear Exercises Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 18 May 2026

  • MND: China Stands Ready to Work With the US Side to Promote the Steady and Long-term Development of a Stable and Positive Mil-to-mil Relationship Global Times 18 May 2026 -- Question: It is reported that during the meeting with the visiting US President Trump, President Xi Jinping pointed out that the two sides should make better use of communication channels in the political, diplomatic and military-to-military fields. What are the expectations on the development of China-US mil-to-mil relations in the next phase?
  • China, Mongolia to hold Steppe Partner 2026 joint army training PRC MND 18 May 2026 -- "China-Mongolia joint army training Steppe Partner 2026 will be held in Inner Mongolia, China, from late May to early June," said Senior Colonel Jiang Bin, spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense (MND), at a press briefing on Monday.

  • INDIAN OCEAN SHIP SAGAR DEPARTS COLOMBO AFTER SUCCESSFUL PORT CALL India PIB 18 May 2026 -- The Indian Navy's Indian Ocean Ship (IOS) Sagar departed Colombo, Sri Lanka on 18 May 2026 following a successful three-day port call, further reinforcing the enduring maritime partnership between India and Sri Lanka and advancing India's vision of MAHASAGAR - Mutual and Holistic Advancement for Security and Growth Across Regions.
  • India - Apache Helicopters Follow-on Support US Dept. of State 18 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of India for its purchase of Apache Helicopters follow-on support services and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $198.2 million.
  • India - Sustainment Support for M777A2 Ultra-Light Howitzers US Dept. of State 18 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of India sustainment support for M777A2 Ultra-Light Howitzers. The estimated total cost is $230 million.

  • Republic of Korea - AH-64E Apache Upgrade Program US Dept. of State 18 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of the Republic of Korea for its AH-64E Apache Upgrade Program. The estimated total cost is $1.2 billion.
  • Republic of Korea - MH-60R Multi-Mission Helicopters US Dept. of State 18 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of the Republic of Korea for its purchase of MH-60R Multi-Mission Helicopters. The estimated total cost is $3.0 billion.

  • Belgium - AGM-184 Joint Strike Missiles US Dept. of State 18 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Belgium for AGM-184 Joint Strike Missiles and related equipment. The estimated total cost is up to $236 million.

  • EU Statement on the occasion of the sixty-ninth session of Working Group A of the Preparatory Commission of the Comprehensive Nuclear Test-Ban Treaty Organisation European External Action Service (EEAS) 18 May 2026

  • Baiba Braže highlights four areas for strengthening European security at a conference in Estonia Latvia MFA 18 May 2026

  • Task on seizing a vessel successfully accomplished in "EFES-2026" exercise Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 18 May 2026 -- The tasks of the next phase were successfully accomplished during "EFES - 2026" Joint Multinational Exercise being held in Türkiye, Azerbaijan`s Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • 18 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 18 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • PM Netanyahu in a conversation with the commander of Flotilla 3 during the takeover of the flotilla that left Turkey Israel - Prime Minister 18 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Netanyahu in a conversation with the commander of Flotilla 3 during the takeover of the flotilla that left Turkey: "You are doing an outstanding job and neutralizing a malicious plan designed to break the isolation we are imposing on Hamas terrorists in Gaza."

  • Eight civilians killed in Israeli strikes on south Lebanon as ceasefire violations mount Press TV 18 May 2026 -- The Lebanese health ministry has reported eight fatalities from the latest Israeli strikes against multiple locations south of Lebanon, marking a fresh flagrant violation of the ceasefire brokered just weeks earlier.

  • Israeli displacement policy in occupied al-Quds poses existential threat to Palestinians: Hamas Press TV 18 May 2026 -- The Hamas resistance movement says a decision by the Israeli occupying regime to expropriate dozens of residential buildings in the vicinity of al-Aqsa Mosque in the Old City of al-Quds is a dangerous step that directly targets the Palestinian existence.

  • Palestine: Killings, destruction and settler encroachment continues UN News 18 May 2026 -- The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas has reduced the scale of violence in the Gaza Strip but killings and destruction continue, while forced displacement in the occupied West Bank has reached a rate "unseen in decades", a senior official with the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said on Monday in Geneva.

  • Joint Statement by the Foreign Ministers of Türkiye, Bangladesh, Brazil, Colombia, Indonesia, Jordan, Libya, Maldives, Pakistan and Spain Regarding the Israeli Assaults on the Global Sumud Flotilla, 18 May 2026 Spain MFA 18 May 2026
  • Turkey condemns Israel's 'new act of piracy' against Gaza aid flotilla Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Turkey has strongly denounced an Israeli attack on the Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla in international waters, describing it as "a new act of piracy."
  • No: 94, 18 May 2026, Regarding the Intervention by Israeli Forces Against the Global Sumud Flotilla Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 18 May 2026
  • Yemen's armed forces promise to stand with Hamas 'until victory' after assassination of Qassam commander Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Yemen's military leadership has firmly reaffirmed its solidarity with Hamas and the Palestinian people, declaring continued joint struggle against the Israeli occupation following the martyrdom of senior Qassam Brigades commander Izz al-Din al-Haddad.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Ukraine, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Protection of Civilians Week UNOCHA 18 May 2026 -- Funding shortfalls squeeze Gaza aid

  • PCG dismisses China's 'stunt' claim, probes Pag-asa incursion Philippine News Agency 18 May 2026 -- Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) spokesperson for the West Philippine Sea Rear Admiral Jay Tarriela on Monday dismissed China's claim that the country's monitoring activities in the WPS are merely "publicity stunts" and has nothing to do with politics.
  • China opposes Philippines' construction activities on all illegally occupied Chinese islands and reefs, will take necessary measures to safeguard Nansha Qundao territorial sovereignty: DM Global Times 18 May 2026 -- China has indisputable sovereignty over the Nansha Qundao (islands) and their adjacent waters. We firmly oppose the Philippines' construction activities on all illegally occupied Chinese islands and reefs, including Zhongye Dao and Mahuan Dao, and will take necessary measures to safeguard territorial sovereignty and maritime rights and interests, Jiang Bin, a spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense, said at a press briefing on Monday when asked to comment on that satellite images reportedly showed that the Philippines is actively upgrading facilities on two islands in the Nansha Qundao, including plans to expand a sheltered harbor on Mahuan Dao and extend the runway on Zhongye Dao.

  • Yemeni armed forces shoot down intruding US MQ-9 Reaper drone Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Yemeni armed forces have intercepted and shot down a US Air Force MQ-9 Reaper drone as it was flying in the skies over the northwestern oil-rich province of Ma'rib.

  • Marking 31 Years Since the Panchen Lama's Disappearance US Dept. of State 18 May 2026 -- Yesterday was the 31st anniversary of the disappearance of the Tibetan religious leader known as the Panchen Lama, Gedhun Choekyi Nyima.

  • Equatorial Guinea Thanks Russia for Sahel Stabilization Efforts - Foreign Minister Sputnik 18 May 2026 -- Equatorial Guinea is grateful to Russia for its efforts to help normalize the situation in Africa's Sahel region, Equatorial Guinean Foreign Minister Simeon Oyono Esono Angue stated on Monday.

News Reports

  • United States Responds to Ebola Outbreak in Africa US Dept. of State 18 May 2026 -- The Department of State is coordinating a comprehensive response to the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda. By working to contain the outbreak before it reaches American shores, the United States is protecting the health of Americans at home and abroad.
  • UNICEF scaling up efforts to protect and support children and families following Ebola outbreaks in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda UNICEF 18 May 2026
  • Ebola outbreak follows hunger and displacement crisis in DR Congo UN News 18 May 2026 -- A day after the World Health Organization (WHO) declared the new Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo an international emergency, top global disease transmission experts stressed that the chances of another global pandemic similar to the 2019 coronavirus emergency are increasing all the time.

  • Address ahead of the official visit to China President of Russia 18 May 2026
  • Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council President of Russia 18 May 2026 -- The President held a briefing session, via videoconference, with permanent members of the Security Council.
  • US renews Russia oil sanctions waiver amid continued Iranian control over Hormuz Press TV 18 May 2026 -- The United States has again extended its Russian oil sanctions waiver amid its inability to cope with rising oil prices, caused by Iran's continued control over the Strait of Hormuz - a key waterway in the Persian Gulf responsible for a fifth of global oil supplies.
  • US Extends Russian Oil Waiver As Bessent Urges Tougher Iran Sanctions RFE/RL 18 May 2026 -- The US Treasury Department has extended for another 30 days a sanctions waiver allowing at-risk countries to purchase Russian oil shipments at sea, as supply disruptions linked to the US-Israeli conflict with Iran continue to roil global energy markets.
  • Shaheen, Warren Statement on Trump Administration Extending Sanctions Relief for Russian Oil Again US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 18 May 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and Elizabeth Warren, Ranking Member of the Senate Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs Committee, issued the below statement condemning the Trump Administration for once again extending the Department of the Treasury's 'temporary' sanctions relief for Russian oil, after General License 134B expired on May 16, 2026.
  • Joint press-release on 16th Meeting of the Russia-Pakistani Consultative Group on Strategic Stability Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 18 May 2026
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Foreign Minister of Tajikistan Sirojiddin Muhriddin Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 18 May 2026
  • Press Release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's talks with Foreign Minister of State for Foreign Affairs, International Cooperation and Diaspora of the Republic of Equatorial Guinea Simeón Oyono Esono Angüe Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 18 May 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's statement and answers to media questions at a joint news conference following talks with Minister of State for Foreign Affairs, International Cooperation and Diaspora of the Republic of Equatorial Guinea Simeón Oyono Esono Angüe, Moscow, May 18, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 18 May 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's opening remarks at talks with Minister of State for Foreign Affairs, International Cooperation and Diaspora of the Republic of Equatorial Guinea Simeón Oyono Esono Angue, Moscow, May 18, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 18 May 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on May 18, 2026 PRC MofA 18 May 2026
  • China ready to work with US to implement important consensus reached by two heads of state: FM on White House fact sheet of Trump's visit to China Global Times 18 May 2026 -- The Chinese side stands ready to work with the US side to fully and faithfully implement the important consensus reached by the two heads of state, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Monday, when asked to comment that the White House released a fact sheet on President Trump's visit to China, outlining the outcomes of the trip.
  • China, Russia to further deepen ties to inject more stability, positive energy into world: Chinese FM briefs on Putin's upcoming state visit to China Global Times 18 May 2026 -- When asked about the relevant arrangements for Russian President Vladimir Putin's upcoming state visit to China, China's expectation, as well as China's view on current China-Russia relations, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Monday that this will mark President Putin's 25th visit to China. During the visit, the two heads of state will exchange views on bilateral ties, cooperation across various fields, as well as international and regional issues of common concern.
  • Chinese FM responds to question on Japanese PM's phone call with Trump en route back from China Global Times 18 May 2026 -- Asked to comment on a question regarding reports that while US President Donald Trump was returning to the US, Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi rushed to hold a phone call with him to reaffirm the importance of the US-Japan alliance and that Japan would also collect information on China-US interactions, including their possible impact on Japan, and making appropriate responses, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Monday that during President Trump's visit to China, the two heads of state conducted in-depth exchanges on regional issues of shared concern. More than 80 years ago, China, the US and other Allied countries fought side by side to defeat Japanese militarism and fascist forces, establishing the post-World War II international order and laying the foundation for peace and development in the Asia-Pacific region.
  • Beijing emerges as focal point of global diplomacy as Putin, Trump visit in succession within a week Global Times 18 May 2026 -- Beijing is fast emerging as the focal point of global diplomacy. China's Foreign Ministry has confirmed that Russian President Vladimir Putin will visit China from May 19 to 20—just days after US President Donald Trump wrapped up his own trip. The tightly sequenced visits have sparked widespread attention, with analysts noting that it is extremely rare in the post-Cold War era for a country to host the leaders of the US and Russia back-to-back within a week.
  • China launches new batch of satellite for Spacesail Constellation Global Times 18 May 2026 -- China launched a Long March-8 carrier rocket on Sunday in the southern island province of Hainan, sending a new satellite group into space.

  • Lai says Taiwan would boost global health role if it joined WHO CNA 18 May 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) said Monday that Taiwan would be able to make greater contributions to global health if it were a member of the World Health Organization (WHO).
  • Taiwan's diplomatic allies advocate for its inclusion in WHA CNA 18 May 2026 -- Senior health officials from three of Taiwan's diplomatic allies on Sunday called for its inclusion in the World Health Assembly (WHA), after Taipei failed to receive an invitation to the 79th session of the annual global health meeting.
  • Taiwan's exclusion from WHA a global health and diplomatic issue: FM CNA 18 May 2026 -- Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung (林佳龍) said Sunday that Taiwan's continued absence from the World Health Assembly (WHA) is not only a global health issue but also a diplomatic one, which is why medical experts from around the world participate in the events that Taiwan usually holds in Geneva each year to coincide with the WHA.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on the 79th WHA's Rejection of the Proposal on Taiwan Global Times 18 May 2026 -- On May 18, the General Committee and the Plenary Session of the 79th World Health Assembly (WHA) respectively decided to reject the so-called proposal of "inviting Taiwan to participate in the WHA as an observer" submitted by certain countries. This has been the 10th year in a row that the WHA has rejected the so-called proposal concerning Taiwan.
  • FM denounces Lai's claims on US non-support for 'Taiwan independence' as attempt to cover up truth Global Times 18 May 2026 -- When asked for comment on Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te's social media response to the US' statement of not supporting "Taiwan independence," in which Lai claimed that Taiwan is a guardian of peace and stability across the Taiwan Straits and the region, and that the "Republic of China" is a sovereign and independent democratic state with no issue of "Taiwan independence," Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun stated on Monday that Lai's far‑fetched claims are a transparent attempt to cover up the truth, laying bare once again his stubborn nature and ulterior motive of pursuing separatist activities for "Taiwan independence."
  • DPP should recognize trend, face reality, pull back before too late: FM spokesperson on Trump's remarks against 'Taiwan independence' Global Times 18 May 2026 -- In response to media inquiry about US President Donald Trump's remarks on the Taiwan question released Friday and the DPP authorities' pro-independence claim, Chinese FM spokesperson Guo Jiakun said, "We have taken note of President Trump's remarks. Taiwan is an inalienable part of China's territory. It has never been a country, and there will never be any possibility of it becoming one in the future."
  • WHA rejects Taiwan-related proposal again; DPP authorities' Geneva gimmick mocked online Global Times 18 May 2026 -- Commenting on the respective decisions by the General Committee and the Plenary Session of the 79th World Health Assembly (WHA) to reject the so-called proposal of "inviting Taiwan to participate in the WHA as an observer" submitted by certain countries, a Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson said on Monday evening that this has been the 10th year in a row that the WHA has rejected the so-called proposal concerning Taiwan. China's Taiwan region, unless given approval by the central government, has no basis, reason or right to participate in the WHA, the spokesperson added.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on the 79th WHA's Rejection of the Proposal on Taiwan PRC MofA 18 May 2026 -- On May 18, the General Committee and the Plenary Session of the 79th World Health Assembly (WHA) respectively decided to reject the so-called proposal of "inviting Taiwan to participate in the WHA as an observer" submitted by certain countries.
  • Trump warning against 'Taiwan independence' sends shockwaves in island; remarks deal a severe blow to DPP and Lai Ching-te: KMT vice chairman Global Times 18 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump's warning against "Taiwan independence" has sent shockwaves through Taiwan's media and political circles. While pro-Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) outlets have largely attempted to downplay the remarks, a vice chairman of the Kuomintang (KMT) characterized the speech as a severe blow to the DPP and Lai Ching-te.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 18 May 2026

  • North Korean leader orders military to reinforce southern border into 'impregnable fortress' Press TV 18 May 2026 -- North Korean leader Kim Jong-un has called for the reinforcement of the front-line units on the country's southern border in an effort to make it into an "impregnable fortress."
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Convenes Meeting of Commanding Officers of Divisions and Brigades of Entire Army and Meets Them KCNA 18 May 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un , General Secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and President of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, convened a meeting of the commanding officers of the divisions and brigades of the entire army on May 17 and met them.

  • Presidents Lee, Trump discuss results of recent US summit in China KOREA.net 18 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump has pledged to play a necessary role and contribute to peace and stability on the Korean Peninsula.

  • EU targets Iran's Revolutionary Guard propaganda ecosystem in an online crackdown EUROPOL 18 May 2026 -- A total of 14 200 posts linked to Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), now designated as a terrorist organisation by the European Union (EU), have been targeted in a coordinated action against terrorist content online.
  • With New Qalibaf Role, Iran Says Beijing Relationship Is Top Priority RFE/RL 18 May 2026 -- Iran has appointed influential parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf as its special representative for China affairs, a move analysts say carries a message that extends well beyond bilateral ties, and may be as much about Washington as it is about Beijing.
  • IRGC intelligence forces capture Mossad spies, bust terror teams across Iran Press TV 18 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) intelligence forces have arrested individuals spying in favor of the Israeli regime's Mossad spy agency, and smashed several terror cells during separate counterterrorism operations across the country.
  • Iranian Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi 'Sent Home' From Hospital RFE/RL 18 May 2026 -- Nobel Peace Prize Winner Narges Mohammadi has been discharged from a Coronary Care Unit (CCU) in Tehran and sent home, according to a statement by her foundation.
  • Iran Executed 'Staggering' Number Of People In 2025, Amnesty Says RFE/RL 18 May 2026 -- Iran executed a "staggering" 2,159 people in 2025, Amnesty International reported on May 18, helping to push the global figure to the highest level since 1981.

  • Netanyahu compels court to cancel his corruption trial due to 'security reasons' Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's testimony has been canceled in his latest corruption trial as his attorneys claimed he had to attend a series of "diplomatic and security meetings."

  • Further U.S. Sanctions on Cuban Regime Elites US Dept. of State 18 May 2026 -- The Trump Administration continues to take decisive action to protect U.S. national security and deprive Cuba's communist regime and military of access to illicit assets.
  • Fact Sheet: Sanctions to Counter Threats Posed by the Cuban Regime US Dept. of State 18 May 2026 -- Today, the Department of State is sanctioning 11 Cuban regime-aligned actors and three entities in furtherance of the Trump Administration's comprehensive campaign to address the pressing national security threats posed by Cuba's communist regime and hold accountable the regime and those who provide it material or financial support.
  • Cuban FM warns US spinning 'fraudulent case' as pretext for invasion Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez has said the United States is fabricating a "fraudulent case" as a pretext for a Cuba invasion.
  • Cuba vows to annihilate US invaders Press TV 18 May 2026 -- Cuba's ambassador and permanent representative to the United Nations has firmly asserted the island nation's sovereign right to defend itself against any potential US military aggression, in line with international law and the UN Charter.

  • Prime Minister holds bilateral talks with Prime Minister of Norway India MEA 18 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi held today bilateral talks with the Prime Minister of Norway, H.E. Mr. Jonas Gahr Støre in Oslo.
  • Transcript of Special Briefing by MEA on the visit of Prime Minister to Sweden (May 17, 2026) India MEA 18 May 2026

  • The Daily Press Briefing (05/18/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 18 May 2026

  • Press conference - Adelaide, South Australia Prime Minister of Australia 18 May 2026

  • The first Japan-Brazil Foreign Ministers' Strategic Dialogue Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 18 May 2026
  • Meeting between Prime Minister TAKAICHI and United Nations Secretary-General Guterres Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 18 May 2026
  • Readout of the Secretary-General's meeting with H.E. Ms. Takaichi Sanae, Prime Minister of Japan UN Secretary-General 18 May 2026

  • Foreign Affairs Council (Development): Press remarks by High Representative Kaja Kallas upon arrival European External Action Service (EEAS) 18 May 2026

  • Homeland Security Task Force Arrests Maduro Regime Ally Alex Saab on Money Laundering Charges Involving Venezuelan Food Contracts and Oil US Dept. of Justice 18 May 2026 -- Venezuela's former Minister of Industry and National Production made his initial appearance in court today pursuant to an indictment unsealed in the Southern District of Florida charging him for his alleged role in a sprawling international money laundering conspiracy involving the corruption and exploitation of a Venezuelan public welfare program intended to provide food to vulnerable Venezuelans.

  • Gitanas Nausėda held a phone call with the President of Moldova President of the Republic of Lithuania 18 May 2026 -- President Gitanas Nausėda spoke by phone on Monday with the President of Moldova, Maia Sandu, at her initiative. The Presidents discussed Moldova's and Ukraine's European integration processes and the next steps toward EU enlargement.

  • "A brand-new path will open up before Türkiye once we get rid of the shackles of terrorism" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 18 May 2026 -- Holding a press conference following the Presidential Cabinet Meeting, President Erdoğan said: "A brand-new path will open up before Türkiye once we get rid of the shackles of terrorism that have been holding our nation back for half a century. As the resources allocated to the fight against terror are channeled to education, health, production and transport, Türkiye's development journey will further speed up."

  • World News in Brief: More attacks in Ukraine, violence against children in Haiti, refugee IDs in Africa UN News 18 May 2026 -- Civilians, including humanitarians, continue to face great danger across war-torn Ukraine amid ongoing hostilities, according to the UN humanitarian relief coordination office there, OCHA.
